summary: Added initial C coverage for `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c`, matching the tiny setter/getter pair `lbHeap_800158D0` and `lbHeap_800158E8` and adding a partial implementation of `lbHeap_80015BD0`. The PR also updated `scratches.txt` statuses for the now-matched scratch entries and adjusted `lbheap.h`/`lbmemory.h` declarations needed by the new code.
what_changed: Created `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c` with includes for `lbmemory.h`, Dolphin OS interrupts, and baselib archive/initialize/memory headers.
Defined the global `int lbHeap_80431FA0[46]` used by the added lbheap routines.
Implemented `lbHeap_800158D0(int arg0, int arg1)` as a write to `lbHeap_80431FA0[arg0 * 7 + 9]`.
Implemented `lbHeap_800158E8(int arg0)` as a read from `lbHeap_80431FA0[arg0 * 7 + 9]`.

Added `lbHeap_80015BD0(s32 arg0, s32 arg1)`, which disables interrupts, branches on fields in `lbHeap_80431FA0`, allocates either through `HSD_MemAlloc` after temporarily switching heaps or through `lbMemory_80014FC8`, optionally converts the returned archive pointer to `header.data_size`, restores interrupts, and returns the result.
Updated `lbheap.h` from the old `MELEE_LB_LBHEAP_H` include guard to `GALE01_0158D0`, added address-commented prototypes, and changed some prototype parameter types from `s32` to `int`.
Added `<baselib/forward.h>` and a prototype for `lbMemory_80014FC8(s32, s32)` to `lbmemory.h`.
Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt`: `lbHeap_800158D0`, `func_800158D0`, and `func_80015BD0` were marked `OK`, replacing previous percentage statuses for these scratch entries.
smart_moves: Kept the two simple accessor routines as direct array-index expressions, preserving the observed `arg0 * 7 + 9` indexing pattern instead of prematurely introducing an unevidenced struct.
Used interrupt bracketing with `OSDisableInterrupts` and `OSRestoreInterrupts` around the allocation logic in `lbHeap_80015BD0`, matching the apparent critical-section behavior.
Added only the external dependency needed from `lbmemory.h` (`lbMemory_80014FC8`) rather than broadening includes unnecessarily.
Used address-style comments in headers, consistent with decomp headers that document symbol addresses next to prototypes.
Updated scratch status metadata alongside the source match so future scratch searches reflect that these entries are now accepted.
decomp_lessons: For early decompilation of data-heavy library code, an indexed global array can be preferable to inventing a struct layout before field meanings are proven.
Tiny accessor functions are useful anchors: matching simple setters/getters can validate global storage layout and array stride before tackling larger functions using the same storage.
When matching allocator code, preserve heap-switching order exactly: save current heap with `HSD_GetHeap`, set target heap, allocate, then restore the saved heap.
Critical sections around allocator/global-state access should be represented explicitly with `OSDisableInterrupts` and `OSRestoreInterrupts` when the assembly indicates it.
Header declarations may need forward headers such as `<baselib/forward.h>` when exposing pointer types like `HSD_Archive*` without pulling full definitions into the header.
The diff shows a partial `lbHeap_80015BD0` report score rather than a full match in the PR table, so future work should treat this implementation as progress but not necessarily final.
naming_conventions: Used `lbHeap_` prefix with address-suffixed names such as `lbHeap_800158D0`, `lbHeap_800158E8`, and `lbHeap_80015BD0`.
Used the global symbol name `lbHeap_80431FA0` for lbheap storage.
Headers used address comments in the form `/* 0158D0 */ void lbHeap_800158D0(int, int);`.
The include guard in `lbheap.h` was changed to the address-style `GALE01_0158D0`, matching the style already present in `lbmemory.h` as `GALE01_014E24`.
Temporary variable names remained compiler/decomp-style (`var_r31`, `temp_r29`, `temp_r30`, `temp_r31`, `temp_r4`), indicating a matching-first implementation rather than a semantic cleanup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Represented pointer arithmetic over `lbHeap_80431FA0` directly with casts and offsets: `((s32*) lbHeap_80431FA0) + (arg0 * 0x1C)` and additional offset checks.
Used raw numeric offsets such as `0x10`, `0x28`, and `4` instead of struct field names, likely to preserve codegen while the layout remains unknown.
Preserved register-like local naming and separate temporaries, a common tactic for improving match fidelity before refactoring.
Matched the accessor functions with simple multiply-plus-offset array indexing, which likely corresponds to the underlying stride in the assembly.

Used an explicit cast through `HSD_Archive*` when assigning `var_r31 = (HSD_Archive*) var_r31->header.data_size;`, reflecting an unusual assembly-level reinterpretation rather than a fully understood type-safe abstraction.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
There is no direct reviewer feedback in the slice; conclusions are based only on the title, report, file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace `lbHeap_80431FA0` raw array indexing with a named struct or documented layout.
Find later work on `lbHeap_80015BD0` to see whether the partial 71.56% score was improved to a full match.
Search for additional callers of `lbHeap_800158D0` and `lbHeap_800158E8` to infer the meaning of slot `arg0 * 7 + 9`.
Search for PRs touching `lbHeap_80015CA8`, `lbHeap_80015D6C`, `lbHeap_80015DF8`, and `lbHeap_80015F3C`, which were still listed as unmatched in the PR report.
Check whether the `lbheap.h` include guard style `GALE01_0158D0` remained stable or was later normalized.
